content: Digestive processes require significant blood flow, diverting 80% away from organs during exercise.
Intestinal permeability caused by minimal blood flow can result in exercise-induced anaphylaxis.
A study found that 52% of Iron Man triathletes who felt nauseous consumed food prior to their race.
Hypertonic beverages have a higher osmotic pressure than bodily fluid and can cause nausea.
Hypotonic beverages have a lower osmotic pressure than bodily fluid and absorb quickly.
Isotonic beverages have an osmotic pressure similar to bodily fluid and absorb at a similar rate.
Consuming isotonic or hypotonic drinks can prevent nausea during exercise.
Omega-3s require mechanical digestion and may not be fully absorbed if consumed close to exercise.
Fruits and vegetables are hard to break down and may not be used as fuel during exercise.
Protein should be fast-absorbing like whey or PE protein.
